title: Bump to v5.0.0-RC4
body:
As of the creation of this PR, we are now change-frozen for Podman v5. Bugfixes only from here on.
Branch to v5.0 is expected late next week, once we complete vendoring of Buildah, containers/storage, containers/image, containers/common.
Target final release date for 5.0 is March 18, assuming no major problems are identified.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body:
Approvals are specifically broken for release PRs, I think. Other approvals work. Release bumps, no work.
@cevich @Luap99 FYI.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** Approval requirements bypassed by manually added approval. This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I would assume because the github diff is empty? That is at least the only thing that is different compared to other PRs.
